title: A Neighborhood Problem
article_text: I used to believe in the justice of our local government, but a recent poll changed my faith. My employer received a formal complaint about the weak streetlights in our neighborhood. The reduction in light was causing a rush of passengers to disappear after dark. One passenger said that the dark streets were a bore and a real problem. I was walking home when I heard a shout from a thin man near the yard. He was trying to compete with a group of people in a rush. Somehow, the tone of the situation made me feel weak. My mission was to reserve a meeting with the employer to discuss the phase of the project. I told him that the proportion of complaints was growing every week. I have been waiting for justice, but my faith is starting to thin.

sentences.en: I used to believe in the justice of our local government, but a recent poll changed my faith.
sentences.zh: 我曾经相信我们当地政府的公正，但最近的民意调查改变了我的信念。

a study of what people think

sentences.en: One passenger said that the dark streets were a bore and a real problem.
